name: Kamala Harris
subject: State of the Union
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
For the past two years under this Administration, the people in power have tried to convince us that the villain in the American story is each other. **That is not our story. That is not who we are. Tonight, before Donald Trump’s State of the Union address, I'm calling on you to join me in the fight for a different American story.** I believe in an America where the economy works for working people. An America where health care is a fundamental right -- not a privilege for those who can afford it. An America where every parent can send their children to school without being haunted by the awful specter of another killing spree. I believe in an America where our daughters, sisters, mothers, and grandmothers are respected. Where reproductive rights are guaranteed in every state in this land. I believe in an America where our democracy and its institutions are protected against all enemies, foreign AND domestic. Where we are focused on making it easier for all eligible voters to cast a ballot -- not harder. **You won’t hear Trump talk about this America tonight -- but that’s the America we believe in; the America I’m ready to fight for.** We have a long road ahead of us, but I know there’s nothing we can’t do if we work together and fight for what’s right. Thanks for all you do, — Kamala

body:
In a few minutes, President Donald J. Trump will travel to the U.S. Capitol to deliver the second State of the Union Address of his presidency. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ddeba967a9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • February 5, 2019
---
| | |
# **TONIGHT: What President Trump will tell Americans**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c07adb007f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **In a few minutes,** President Donald J. Trump will travel to the U.S. Capitol to deliver the second State of the Union Address of his presidency. [Watch it live at 9 p.m. ET.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2c47801262&e=3ee4985eea)

**Here’s a preview** of what he’ll tell Congress and the American people:

_“Together, we can break decades of political stalemate. We can bridge old divisions, heal old wounds, build new coalitions, forge new solutions, and unlock the extraordinary promise of America’s future. The decision is ours to make.”_

**The President picked** one simple, powerful message for our country tonight: “Choosing Greatness.” When our leaders in Washington put citizens first and their careers second—when they choose a spirit of compromise over the politics of retribution—there is no limit to what America can achieve.

**Both parties can rally** behind this vision. We can protect American workers by passing the U.S.–Mexico–Canada Agreement to replace NAFTA. We can rebuild our country with a bipartisan infrastructure package that puts people to work. We can rebuild trust with a safe, legal immigration system that halts the flow of drugs and crime into our country.

**President Trump was elected** to solve the problems that Washington gave up trying to fix. He is a dealmaker offering common-sense, bipartisan solutions. In the past few months, Republicans and Democrats have already come together to pass historic criminal justice reform and crucial legislation to fund the war on opioid abuse.

**These issues matter.** Americans don’t want resistance. They want results.

[Watch President Trump’s second State of the Union live at 9 p.m. ET.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f108cdca3&e=3ee4985eea)

**Behind the scenes:** [Meet the First Lady’s special guests for tonight’s speech](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=85406fae70&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

name: Mary Mancini
subject: Last night we glimpsed our future
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Peter,

Last night, Donald Trump's State of the Union was all smoke, mirrors, and empty rhetoric.

But the Trump sideshow slowly faded into the background when we saw the Democratic women of Congress dressed in white to commemorate women's suffrage movement and heard Stacey Abrams make history as the first black woman to give the nationally televised official Democratic response.

This is our future and nothing can diminish its importance or tarnish its shine.

Tennessee is facing a critical moment in its history. Introducing a bold economic agenda at every level of governance, like Ms. Abrams outlined, is an enormous progressive endeavor, and our efforts depend on the grassroots power of Tennessee Democrats like you.

[_C_ an we count on your help?](https://act.myngp.com/el/F1oB3ofLIifMYy9q8XwsJxCQyscpfrtRsA86kRMg48M=/OGRru78kuPUqirCbz5-lzB1b8WKrr0qEu0iC4zcBtv8=)

The hard work we do today to fight for the future of our people, our economy, the soul of our country is the most important work we can do. Your donation will be put to work to help amplify our Democratic message focused on real solutions for working families and recruit more Democrats that represent our future - like Stacy Abrams.

Please join us and help us change Tennessee for the better.

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/F1oB3ofLIifMYy9q8XwsJxCQyscpfrtRsA86kRMg48M=/VB-k1dSQ_2h5Lw8HMOyPVbm2zr0vFj-27qa-gJSYGGU=) | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/F1oB3ofLIifMYy9q8XwsJxCQyscpfrtRsA86kRMg48M=/7BbdCH3Zn1zwIHZfESze8CufLTwpz-Y4t_ba_kUCzsY=) | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/F1oB3ofLIifMYy9q8XwsJxCQyscpfrtRsA86kRMg48M=/p6wv87KJILe0M7L_U_IdJERAOQl1zA6fUeyVY2A3NTQ=) | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/F1oB3ofLIifMYy9q8XwsJxCQyscpfrtRsA86kRMg48M=/OGRru78kuPUqirCbz5-lzB1b8WKrr0qEu0iC4zcBtv8=)

Thank you for your continued support.

Mary

subject: With Pitch for Unity, President Trump Urges Republicans and Democrats to ‘Choose Greatness’
body:
"President Trump in his State of the Union address Tuesday night issued a call for unity and an end to the political divisiveness..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8e6d33c12d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **With Pitch for Unity, President Trump Urges Republicans and Democrats to ‘Choose Greatness’**

“President Trump in his State of the Union address Tuesday night issued a call for unity and an end to the political divisiveness that has ensnared Washington,” Melissa Quinn reports for the _Washington Examiner_.

During the speech—with its theme of “choosing greatness”—President Trump laid out five priorities that should unite both parties in Washington: “American jobs and fair trade, rebuilding the nation’s infrastructure, reducing the price of healthcare and prescription drugs, creating a safe and lawful immigration system, and pursuing a foreign policy agenda that ‘puts America’s interests first.’”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ffd3a7b11f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=60116e1097&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Tal Axelrod reports that roughly three-quarters of American voters approved of President Trump’s State of the Union address, according to CNN and CBS News polls. “Seventy six percent approved of the speech in the CBS poll, with 24 percent saying they disapproved,” Axelrod writes. But the telling stat: CBS found that “about 82 percent of independents in that survey who watched the speech liked what they heard.”
---
|
---
| “The speech was big, not just in length — about 80 minutes — but also in concept. It had a structure. It had a message. It had passages to appeal to all Americans. It had passages to appeal to Trump's conservative base. And it had passages to appeal to opposition Democrats,” Byron York writes in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4662cf560e&e=3ee4985eea)_. “He delivered a big, broad, far-ranging statement of his approach to the presidency and to the country.”
---
|
---
| “The president also took time to highlight a few of the many incredible achievements under his administration” in last night’s State of the Union, Kimberly Guilfoyle writes in _[The Daily Caller](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=646d132830&e=3ee4985eea). _“Record-low unemployment rates for African Americans, Hispanics, and women. Rising wages and tax cuts for working-class families. A historic bipartisan criminal justice reform bill to give non-violent offenders a second chance.”
---
|
---
| “Members of Congress broke out into a spirited singing of ‘Happy Birthday’ for a guest of President Trump’s State of the Union who survived both the Holocaust and the anti-Semitic mass shooting at a Pittsburgh synagogue,” Tamar Lapin reports in the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c9e97cc826&e=3ee4985eea). President Trump also honored three veterans who served in World War II: Private First Class Joseph Reilly, Staff Sergeant Irving Locker, and Sergeant Herman Zeitchik.
---
|

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: Our work is not done
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Action
body:
We need a system where voters pick their leaders - not where politicians pick their voters. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa645/34a064cc/730734f/703be31/1948653433/VEsH/ "https://www.fairfightgeorgia.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=abrams&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=IE020619SOTUfull&source=IE020619SOTUfull")

| | | Friend, Last night, I was honored to deliver the State of the Union response. I was humbled by the opportunity to give voice to the needs and dreams of our fellow Americans. What our movement proved last year is that we can and we must rise together. Because we share the same goal for our country, and that is progress, that is opportunity. But we cannot gain that opportunity if we cannot get a fair fight, if even a single eligible voter cannot access the ballot. [**Today, I ask you to join me in our demand for a fair fight. Can I count on you to make a $3 contribution today? Together, we will make sure that every voice and every vote gets counted. **](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa645/34a064cc/730734f/703be3e/1948653433/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE020619SOTU-full&utm_medium=email&utm_source=abrams&utm_content=2+-+Today+I+ask+you+to+join+me+in+our+demand&utm_campaign=IE020619SOTUfull&source=IE020619SOTUfull") Because when democracy fails our people, we must be willing to lift our voices and challenge the choices of those who say they speak for us. As I said last night, voter suppression is real. From making it harder to register and stay on the rolls to closing polling places or putting shoddy equipment in poor communities to rejecting lawfully cast ballots, we can no longer ignore these threats to democracy. So our work is not done. Our work is not done because Medicaid has not been expanded. Our work is not done because Americans with disabilities still face discrimination and barriers where they live and work. Our work is not done because too many Americans lack access to a quality, public education that invests in every child from cradle to career. We must continue our fight to create a nation where our dreams and aspirations become real and lasting legacies. But to get there, we need a system where voters pick their leaders - not where politicians pick their voters. [**Time is not waiting on us. Change is not waiting on us. A new day is dawning in Georgia and in our country. Will you make a contribution today, and join me as we demand a fair fight?**](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa645/34a064cc/730734f/703be3f/1948653433/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/fairfightga2018-2?refcode=IE020619SOTU-full&utm_medium=email&utm_source=abrams&utm_content=3+-+Time+is+not+waiting+on+us+Change+is+not+&utm_campaign=IE020619SOTUfull&source=IE020619SOTUfull") Onward,
Stacey

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: A bit of an eye roll
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
body:
Another SOTU with no vision
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976937/2ae112ef/17ef3027/357a7e5b/3291713231/VEsH/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976937/2ae112ef/17ef3027/357a7e5b/3291713231/VEsE/ "https://2020.kirstengillibrand.com/")
Peter, Trump's State of the Union didn't come with any policies to make people's lives better, and he definitely didn't have a vision for our future. Instead, he came with the same old false lines of attack. It was all a bit of an ... eye roll.
* He celebrated the record number of female jobs he created, except he doesn't seem to understand that the female jobs he created were Democratic women in Congress.
* He attacked a woman's right to make her own health decisions and suggested, as he does almost every day on Twitter, that independent investigations into his corruption and ties to Russia should be shut down.
* He demonized immigrants, went on and on about his wasteful wall and called border crossings an "urgent national crisis."

If the vision of America you heard last night isn't yours, let's come together and defeat him. Where he offers nothing but division and fear, we'll run on compassion and courage. And if you're with me, you can help build a campaign to do it by chipping in now:

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: Bernie Sanders is absolutely right
body:
There is one thing President Trump did not say last night -- that the state of the union has never looked better for corporate CEOs, Wall Street bankers, and the billionaire class.
# [](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5db/bed43eb/9e25cb74/75fbe40f/3166501512/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-header-OR")
Friends,
There is one thing President Trump did not say last night -- that the state of the union has never looked better for corporate CEOs, Wall Street bankers, and the billionaire class.
For the rest of us, the state of the union is in peril.
The median American family has lost 3 percent of its wealth, while the richest 0.1 percent have grown their wealth by 133 percent. The gap between the very rich and everyone else is wider than at any time since the Gilded Age.
**America is now a world leader on wealth and income inequality.** The good news is that a majority of Americans agree that the billionaire class is not paying their fair share. A recent poll showed that 63 percent of Americans say that rich people are paying too little in taxes.
Defeating the billionaire class' stronghold begins with beating Trump in 2020, but it does not stop there.
[**Chip in $5 right now to support Our Revolution 's grassroots organizing efforts to urge Senator Bernie Sanders to run for president in 2020.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5db/bed43eb/9e25cb74/75fbe40e/3166501512/VEsE/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-sotu?refcode=em190206-d&noskip=true")
The only way we are going to reverse the corruption of our electoral system is by electing someone who has never accepted a single dime from the billionaire class -- someone with the vision and the plan for a more socially, racially, politically, and economically equitable country.
[**We need to elect a candidate who is not afraid to take on Wall Street -- a candidate who is willing to fight for the needs of the American people, Black, brown, and white.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a5db/bed43eb/9e25cb74/75fbe40e/3166501512/VEsF/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/or-sotu?refcode=em190206-d&noskip=true")
If we act right now, we will be a part of a historic presidential candidacy that will confront the trends of wealth and income inequality head-on and give every single woman, man, and child in this country a brighter future.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner
President
Our Revolution

"" The only way we are going to reverse the corruption of our electoral system is by electing someone who has never accepted a single dime from the billionaire class -- someone with the vision and the plan for a more socially, racially, politically, and economically equitable country. "" If we act right now, we will be a part of a historic presidential candidacy that will confront the trends of wealth and income inequality head-on and give every single woman, man, and child in this country a brighter future. | Last night, President Donald J. Trump delivered his State of the Union address. And we've got BIG NEWS following the speech.

**Flash polls from two major media outlets both show that 76% of Americans who watched the speech viewed it positively.**

CNN's breakdown -- yes, CNN -- even shows that 59% of viewers had a _very_ positive reaction to the speech.

|
Overwhelmingly, Americans decided that they liked what they heard from President Trump directly:
* _They liked what they heard when President Trump spoke about America's booming economy -- with records jobs, rising wages and slashed regulations._

* **_They liked what they heard when President Trump spoke about eliminating the Obamacare individual mandate, and lowering the cost of healthcare and prescription drugs. _**

* _They liked what they heard when President Trump spoke about rebuilding our nation's infrastructure. _

* _**They liked what they heard when President Trump said he would never apologize for advancing America's interests. **_

* _They liked what they heard when President Trump said America would never be a socialist nation._

* **_And they liked what they heard when President Trump made it clear that the time is _NOW_ to secure our nation's southern border. _**

**\-- > Short version: They liked what they heard! -->**

Under President Trump's leadership, America is strong economically and globally. President Trump continues to deliver for ALL Americans with extraordinary bipartisan achievements, and he's solving problems with common sense solutions. That's something we can all rally around.

name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: President Trump's address
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
Last night, I joined my fellow Americans in watching the President's State of the Union address.
While I appreciate that he touched on some important issues facing hard-working Americans, including the need to lower prescription drug costs, much of what I heard was a vision that does more to divide us than to bring our country together.
I was particularly concerned that the President failed to rule out shutting down the government again.
One thing is clear: we have a long road ahead of us as we work to address our country's most pressing challenges.
**I 'm committed to working with anyone and everyone who's serious about getting results. [This won't be easy, and I need to know that you're with me. Will you add your name to recommit to our fight for progress?](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc149/2a380d31/35070606/f10a8d/3606604357/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20190206_SOTUresponse?source=e20190206_SOTUresponse_up&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")**

name: Tom O'Halleran
subject: talk is cheap...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom O'Halleran for Congress
body:
We need to make bipartisanship work again folks.
Tom O'Halleran is running for Congress in Arizona's 1st district, a critical race to getting Congress back on track. Click here to donate to Tom's campaign and help him put Arizona families first.
[](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d633c3e/1a8421e1/19bc6ae97/5d475184/2893139710/VEsH/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ohalleranq12019?refcode=em_header&recurring=auto&amount=5&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000")
---
We need to make bipartisanship work again folks. Over the past few years, we've seen a downward spiral that threatens the efficiency of our government. There have been shutdowns, endless filibustering, and manipulation of the rules to suit political needs. **But what 's falling by the wayside is the American people.** While politicians bicker over policy points, Americans are losing health insurance. While ideologues heckle their opponents on Twitter, thousands of American families are suffering and dying from drug abuse. And while talk-show politics have become mainstream talking points, DREAMers -- who know and love no other country but the United States -- are being detained. **The president spoke bold words about working across the aisle, but talk is cheap -- he needs to show he means it with real, comprehensive action.** I'm ready to work with anyone to protect folks with preexisting conditions. I'll gladly lock arms with anyone who's serious about getting American families the support they need to tackle the opioid epidemic. And my door is always open to politicians who will throw rhetoric to the roadside, to protect undocumented Americans who came to this country as children. [_**Sign my petition, and signal to the White House and Congress that the First District is ready for a return to efficient, effective government that works for Arizona and the rest of the country.**_](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d633c3e/1a8421e1/19bc6ae97/5d475185/2893139710/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg1MzA3In0=/ "http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/s/talk-is-cheap?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=85307") Thank you, Tom | ****[DONATE](http://action.tomohalleran. clean_body: We need to make bipartisanship work again folks. Tom O'Halleran is running for Congress in Arizona's 1st district, a critical race to getting Congress back on track. Click here to donate to Tom's campaign and help him put Arizona families first. "" We need to make bipartisanship work again folks. Over the past few years, we've seen a downward spiral that threatens the efficiency of our government. There have been shutdowns, endless filibustering, and manipulation of the rules to suit political needs. But what 's falling by the wayside is the American people. While politicians bicker over policy points, Americans are losing health insurance. While ideologues heckle their opponents on Twitter, thousands of American families are suffering and dying from drug abuse. And while talk-show politics have become mainstream talking points, DREAMers -- who know and love no other country but the United States -- are being detained. The president spoke bold words about working across the aisle, but talk is cheap -- he needs to show he means it with real, comprehensive action. I'm ready to work with anyone to protect folks with preexisting conditions. I'll gladly lock arms with anyone who's serious about getting American families the support they need to tackle the opioid epidemic. And my door is always open to politicians who will throw rhetoric to the roadside, to protect undocumented Americans who came to this country as children. "" Thank you, Tom | ** "=" Representative Tom O 'Halleran represents Arizona's First Congressional District in the 115th Congress. As a former police officer, small businessman, and Arizona state legislator, Tom knows what it takes to bring people together and bring positive change to Arizona families.
